But that doesn't matter.

body:
It's not allow to change `buildInfo` during code generation.

After a single build code generation could run multiple times. Modifying it would leave weird side effects.

Instead create a new `Set` and expose that as code generation result. We have a `data` map in the code generation result where this can be places `data.set("topLevelDeclarations", topLevelDeclarations)`
author: sokra

body: That's not correct. This PR should only result in less inlining.
author: sokra

body:
removing that is not correct.

When at least one module doesn't provide topLevelDeclarations we can't tell about the topLevelDeclarations of the concatenated module, so it should be `undefined` (which means "no information")
author: sokra

body:
> And I also think the if (RESERVED_NAMES.has(decl)) continue; is incorrect here. What do you think?

maybe basic idea was that `buildInfo.topLevelDeclarations` are useless in this case since renaming will happen anyway, but yeah this is not correct (will lead to error only in a case of assets. since no populating assets happens on `continue`).
author: vankop

body:
Instead of using both topLevelDeclarations maybe it's better to use

``` js
const topLevelDeclarations =
			(data && data.get("topLevelDeclarations")) ||
			(module.buildInfo && module.buildInfo.topLevelDeclarations);
```

So the topLevelDeclarations from code generations overrides the original topLevelDeclarations, since it's basically a better version of it. It includes renames, which means some original topLevelDeclarations might be no longer in the code generation topLevelDeclarations, e. g. all reserved names.
author: sokra

body: Assuming it should also be possible that `buildInfo.topLevelDeclarations` is not set but `data.topLevelDeclarations` is, we also need to improve that piece of code: https://github.com/webpack/webpack/blob/13a3bc13736e3b7066779623939e9fdaec7bc06b/lib/javascript/JavascriptModulesPlugin.js#L1107-L1108

`infrastructureLogErrors.length &&` is required in current design since errors should be checked only on `run > 1`

other alternatives as I see:
- use `infrastructure-log1.js`, `infrastructure-log2.js`, `infrastructure-log3.js` files

- don't use `checkArrayExpectation`, use regexps in `infrastructure-log.js` to filter log messages and then only check for errors.

body:
```suggestion
	getSource(sourceString, compilation) {
```

and call `getSource` with `compilation`.

But you will notice that you don't have a `compilation` in `codeGeneration`, so add that to the `CodeGenerationContext` as **optional** property (optional to not make it a breaking change).

In `Compilation.codeGeneration()` pass the `compilation` property to the context.

`associatedObjectForCache` is optional, so it's fine to omit it when `compilation` is not provided.

Add a TODO comment to `CodeGenerationContext` to make `compilation` required in webpack 6

body:
the reason is that in line https://github.com/webpack/webpack/blob/dfdc8b133d3d49371251e29efeefb9d31b982c9e/lib/SourceMapDevToolPlugin.js#L97
all modules will use `compilation.options.context` to get absolute path
author: cool-little-fish

body:
module context is where nested modules resolves. e.g.
you have files
```
/a/b/c
/a/b/b
```
then request
```js
import(`/a/b/${some}`)
```
will result in context `/a/b` (if no other resolving happen) since all submodules will be resolved related to this context => `./c`, `./b` (`/a/b/c` and `/a/b/b` respectively)
author: vankop

body:
that's right, but the case is different here, because there is module source name mapping, all module generated by webpack which name starts with `webpack://` will get its absolute path to find its module object. see,
https://github.com/webpack/webpack/blob/dfdc8b133d3d49371251e29efeefb9d31b982c9e/lib/SourceMapDevToolPlugin.js#L98
author: cool-little-fish

body:
yeah. I was talking about moving that output from here (DefaultStatsFactoryPlugin) to `DefaultStatsPrinterPlugin` as it's related to printing not to capturing data.

DefaultStatsFactoryPlugin converts from webpack classes to JSON
DefaultStatsPrinterPlugin converts from JSON to string output

In this case:

DefaultStatsFactoryPlugin should return something like `{ ..., details: "abcd", filteredDetails: 2 }`.
and DefaultStatsPrinterPlugin should format that as `abcd \n + 2 hidden lines`

Here something similar:

https://github.com/webpack/webpack/blob/870fcadfd01beec431e598968f237b59350e475a/lib/stats/DefaultStatsPrinterPlugin.js#L632-L633

body:
When `value` is `undefined` or `null` we need to fallback to determining the value at runtime.

This could be the case e. g. in the following cases:

* `providedExports` is disabled
* `import * as x from "./commonjs.js"; "a" in x;`
* `import * as x from "./esm.js"; "a" in x.a.b.c;`

Then you want to generate something like `"usedName" in a.b.c.d`

body:
sync is not possible for `import()`

and `async-weak` is internal. `weak` will lead to `async-weak` automatically when using `import()`

`lazy-once` is only available for an `import()` with expression.

So this should be `eager | weak | lazy | lazy-once`

And `lazy-once` should be accepted for import() without expression too, and behave as `lazy`
author: sokra

body: I think we can add support to any type, we just need to load source map when `loadSourceMap` is true and pass loaded source map to loader(s), currently solution lose source map from loader, loader can generate additional code and change source map
author: alexander-akait

body:
>I think we can add support to any type, we just need to load source map when loadSourceMap is true and pass loaded source map to loader(s)

lets add this to parser options.

> loader can generate additional code and change source map

need @sokra opinion on that.. In this case I think we should keep loader solution. Core should support only source map extraction after loaders run. Since otherwise we still lose source map, if e.g. `babel-loader` will generate another source map.
author: vankop

body:
I also think that we should extract the source map before loaders run.

We can assume (or have to) that loaders handle source map themselves. (They get passed input source map and can return generated source map)

Do we can handle a source map when reading the resource. In NormalModule, where the resource is read.

This might require some changes in loader-runner, since they need to allow returning multiple arguments from `readResource`, or something like that.

We have to think about binary vs string representation of resources. We don't want to do Source Map extraction of binary resources, either do we want to unnecessary convert binary to string. So I guess we want to have a readResource callback where we know if the next loader expects binary or string.

---

(A loader generating a `sourceMappingURL` comment is an incorrect loader, since that's not the way loaders should return a source map. But in that case users can still "fix" the loader by adding the `source-map-loader`, but that's still an exception)
author: sokra

body: I'm not sure which option use/add otherwise..
author: vankop

body:
should we:
- flatten source map if there is `map.sections`
- try to read source content for sources which doesn't have it in `map.sourceContent[i]`
author: vankop

body:
I have refactored design:
- extract source map before loaders run ( while processing resource in `loader-runner` )
- try to extract source map **only** when option `parserOptions.extractSourceMap===true`
- when extracting explicitly convert resource content to string
author: vankop

body:
> * flatten source map if there is `map.sections`

That's really really uncommon. I would even stay nobody is using these types of SourceMaps.

> * try to read source content for sources which doesn't have it in `map.sourceContent[i]`

Yes, that would make sense. Also make sure to add these files as `this.addDependency`
author: sokra

body:
parser option sounds wrong here, since this is independent from parsing and also doesn't happen in the same phase.

I would go with a module level options `configuration.module.extractSourceMap` or probably better `Rule.extractSourceMap`.
author: sokra

body:
`.split()` is very expensive. I'd recommend using repeated calls to `lastIndexOf('\n', lastNewline)`, especially since this regexp only needs to match the last one.

Though it might also make sense to run a `lastIndexOf('sourceMappingURL')` as a sanity check first.
author: dmichon-msft

body:
Based on feedback during development of https://github.com/webpack-contrib/source-map-loader/, better to make an options for this, in real world a lot of packages have broken source maps and developer can't fix it in vendor libraries, so better generate a warning, because it really doesn't break a build, but allow to setup it to make an error, i.e.

```ts
type ExtractSourceMap = boolean | { errorSeverity: "none" | "warn" | "error" }
```
author: alexander-akait

body: Let's add a test for remote source maps, i.e. `http://github.com/webpack/webpack/test/configCases/source-map/my-source-map.js.map`

body: Does we support `sections`? like here https://github.com/webpack-contrib/source-map-loader/blob/master/src/index.js#L91 because I see them sometimes
author: alexander-akait

body:
Also let's implement https://github.com/webpack-contrib/source-map-loader/tree/master?tab=readme-ov-file#filtersourcemappingurl, sometimes some source maps are really broken and you can't do anything with them, so let's add:
```
type ExtractSourceMap = boolean | {  errorSeverity: "...", test: Rules, include: Rules, Exlcude: Rules }
```
author: alexander-akait

Looks good, but I can't see where `context` is used when passing a string to createRequire.

Also it might make sense to rename `context` to `issuer` since `context` usually points to a directory in webpack context, while `issuer` would be the requesting module.

I think we evaluate `import.meta` to an identifier in `evaluateExpression`, so this special case isn't needed in my opinion.

You can use `evaluated.isIdentifier() && evaluated.identifier === "import.meta.url"` for that case in the code below
